title: Null Island

infobox.value: is the location at zero degrees [latitude](/wiki/Latitude "Latitude") and zero degrees [longitude](/wiki/Longitude "Longitude") ([0°N 0°E﻿ / ﻿0°N 0°E﻿ / 0; 0](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Null_Island&params=0_N_0_E_type:isle)), i.e., where the [prime meridian](/wiki/IERS_Reference_Meridian "IERS Reference Meridian") and the [equator](/wiki/Equator "Equator") intersect. Since there is no landmass located at these coordinates, it is not an actual [island](/wiki/Island "Island"). The name is often used in mapping software as a placeholder to help find and correct database entries that have erroneously been assigned the coordinates 0,0. Although "Null Island" started as a joke within the [geospatial](/wiki/Geospatial "Geospatial") community, it has become a useful means of addressing a recurring issue in [geographic information science](/wiki/Geographic_information_science "Geographic information science").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Juhasz, Levente; Mooney, Peter (2022). [""I Think i Discovered a Military Base in the Middle of the Ocean"—Null Island, the Most Real of Fictional Places"](https://doi.org/10.1109%2FACCESS.2022.3197222). *IEEE Access*. **10**: 84147–84165. [arXiv](/wiki/ArXiv_(identifier) "ArXiv (identifier)"):[2204.08383](https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.08383). [Bibcode](/wiki/Bibcode_(identifier) "Bibcode (identifier)"):[2022IEEEA..1084147J](https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2022IEEEA..1084147J). [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1109/ACCESS.2022.3197222](https://doi.org/10.1109%2FACCESS.2022.3197222). [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [2169-3536](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/2169-3536).

Jacobs, Frank (March 25, 2022). ["Welcome to Null Island, where lost data goes to die"](https://bigthink.com/strange-maps/null-island/). *Big Think*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20221209153114/https://bigthink.com/strange-maps/null-island/) from the original on 2022-12-09. Retrieved 2022-12-13.

[*Map of the Gold Coast Colony and neighbouring territories*](https://britishlibrary.georeferencer.com/maps/04591550-c132-54a4-8872-0408b8e9efe0/) (Map). London: [War Office](/wiki/War_Office "War Office"), Intelligence Division. IDWO 1097 – via Georeferencer.

Hotz, Robert Lee (14 July 2016). ["If You Can't Follow Directions, You'll End Up on Null Island"](https://www.wsj.com/articles/if-you-cant-follow-directions-youll-end-up-on-null-island-1468422251). *[The Wall Street Journal](/wiki/The_Wall_Street_Journal "The Wall Street Journal")*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20160713161209/http://www.wsj.com/articles/if-you-cant-follow-directions-youll-end-up-on-null-island-1468422251) from the original on 13 July 2016. Retrieved 14 July 2016.

Currie, Chris [@chriscurrie] (18 April 2009). ["@braz we call that spot 'Null Island.' http://tr.im/j5nI It's at Lat 0, Lon 0"](https://twitter.com/chriscurrie/status/1546199025) ([Tweet](/wiki/Tweet_(social_media) "Tweet (social media)")). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20180910184441/https://twitter.com/chriscurrie/status/1546199025) from the original on 10 September 2018. Retrieved 4 August 2022 – via [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter").

St. Onge, Tim (9 May 2016). ["Null Island is One of the Most Visited Places on Earth. Too Bad It Doesn't Exist"](http://www.atlasobscura.com/articles/null-island-is-one-of-the-most-visited-places-on-earth-too-bad-it-doesnt-exist). *Atlas Obscura*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20170825062049/http://www.atlasobscura.com/articles/null-island-is-one-of-the-most-visited-places-on-earth-too-bad-it-doesnt-exist) from the original on 25 August 2017. Retrieved 4 August 2022.

Aric, Toler (29 January 2018). ["How to Use and Interpret Data from Strava's Activity Map"](https://www.bellingcat.com/resources/how-tos/2018/01/29/strava-interpretation-guide/). *[Bellingcat](/wiki/Bellingcat "Bellingcat")*. Retrieved 2 February 2018.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [0°N 0°E﻿ / ﻿0°N 0°E﻿ / 0; 0](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Null_Island&params=0_N_0_E_type:isle)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Cartographical (only) marker at 0°N, 0°E

Null Island

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Null Island is located in Africa](/wiki/File:Africa_location_map.svg "Null Island is located in Africa") Null Island  Null Island  The point of intersection of the prime meridian and the equator, in the Gulf of Guinea | |
| Geography | |
| Coordinates | [0°N 0°E﻿ / ﻿0°N 0°E﻿ / 0; 0](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Null_Island&params=0_N_0_E_type:isle) |

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4a/Commons-logo.svg/40px-Commons-logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Commons-logo.svg)

Wikimedia Commons has media related to [Null Island](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Null_Island "commons:Category:Null Island").

**Null Island** is the location at zero degrees [latitude](/wiki/Latitude "Latitude") and zero degrees [longitude](/wiki/Longitude "Longitude") ([0°N 0°E﻿ / ﻿0°N 0°E﻿ / 0; 0](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Null_Island&params=0_N_0_E_type:isle)), i.e., where the [prime meridian](/wiki/IERS_Reference_Meridian "IERS Reference Meridian") and the [equator](/wiki/Equator "Equator") intersect. Since there is no landmass located at these coordinates, it is not an actual [island](/wiki/Island "Island"). The name is often used in mapping software as a placeholder to help find and correct database entries that have erroneously been assigned the coordinates 0,0. Although "Null Island" started as a joke within the [geospatial](/wiki/Geospatial "Geospatial") community, it has become a useful means of addressing a recurring issue in [geographic information science](/wiki/Geographic_information_science "Geographic information science").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Physical location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Null_Island&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Physical location")]

The point on the Earth's surface defined as Null Island is located in [international waters](/wiki/International_waters "International waters") in the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), roughly 600 kilometres (320 nmi) south of the [West African](/wiki/West_Africa "West Africa") coast in the [Gulf of Guinea](/wiki/Gulf_of_Guinea "Gulf of Guinea").[[2]](#cite_note-onge-2) The depth of the seabed there is around 4,940 m (16,200 ft).[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-noaa-4)

The closest land to Null Island is a small islet 570 km (354 mi; 307.8 nmi) to the north ([4°45′30″N 1°58′33″W﻿ / ﻿4.75833°N 1.97583°W﻿ / 4.75833; -1.97583](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Null_Island&params=4_45_30_N_1_58_33_W_))[*[failed verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] that is part of [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana "Ghana"). The nearest point on the mainland is Achowa Point[[5]](#cite_note-5) between [Akwidaa](/wiki/Akwidaa "Akwidaa") and [Dixcove](/wiki/Dixcove "Dixcove"), both in Ghana.

## In software

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Null_Island&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: In software")]

In terms of computing and placename databases, the coordinates for Null Island were added to the [Natural Earth](/wiki/Natural_Earth "Natural Earth") public domain map dataset[[6]](#cite_note-Kurgan-6)[[7]](#cite_note-NED-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) c. 2010–2011, after which the term came into wide use (although there is evidence of it being used previously).[[9]](#cite_note-9)[*[user-generated source](/wiki/Wikipedia:Identifying_reliable_sources#User-generated_content "Wikipedia:Identifying reliable sources")*] Since then, the "island" has, through fiction, been given a geography, history, and flag.[[2]](#cite_note-onge-2) Natural Earth describes the entity as a "1 meter square island" (11 sq ft) with "scale rank 100, indicating it should never be shown in mapping".[[6]](#cite_note-Kurgan-6) The name "Null" refers to the two zero coordinates, as [null](/wiki/Null_(mathematics) "Null (mathematics)") values (indicating an absence of data) are often coerced to a value of 0 when converted to an integer context or "no-nulls allowed" [context](/wiki/Check_constraint#NOT_NULL_constraint "Check constraint").

The location is used by mapping systems to [trap errors](/wiki/Exception_handling "Exception handling").[[7]](#cite_note-NED-7) Such errors arise, for example, where an image artifact is erroneously associated to the location by software which cannot attribute a geoposition, and instead associates a latitude and longitude of "Null,Null" or "0,0".[[10]](#cite_note-10) As reported in January 2018 by [Bellingcat](/wiki/Bellingcat "Bellingcat"), other data mapped to the location include activity events from the [Strava](/wiki/Strava "Strava") fitness-tracking app, apparently mapped to the location due to users entering "0,0" coordinates to disguise their real locations.[[11]](#cite_note-Toler-11)

## Soul buoy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Null_Island&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Soul buoy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/Null_Island_2017.jpg/250px-Null_Island_2017.jpg)](/wiki/File:Null_Island_2017.jpg)

The weather buoy formerly moored at the coordinates of Null Island, 0°N 0°E

A [weather buoy](/wiki/Weather_buoy "Weather buoy"), named the Soul buoy after the [soul music](/wiki/Soul_music "Soul music") genre, was moored at this location.[[12]](#cite_note-Liberation-12) The buoy ("Station 13010 – Soul") was part of the [PIRATA](/wiki/Prediction_and_Research_Moored_Array_in_the_Atlantic "Prediction and Research Moored Array in the Atlantic") system, a set of 17 buoys installed in the tropical Atlantic Ocean since 1997 by the United States, France, and Brazil.[[13]](#cite_note-ndbc-13) Like the other buoys in the system, it was named after a musical genre.[[12]](#cite_note-Liberation-12) It was an Autonomous Temperature Line Acquisition System (ATLAS) buoy that was conical in shape and 3.8 metres (12 ft) high. It was anchored by a cable to the seabed.[[4]](#cite_note-noaa-4)

The buoy disappeared less than a year after its installation, and was replaced in 1998.[[12]](#cite_note-Liberation-12) The buoy was decommissioned in March 2021.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Null_Island&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* *[Colonel Bleep](/wiki/Colonel_Bleep "Colonel Bleep")* – a 1957 [animated cartoon](/wiki/Animated_cartoon "Animated cartoon") that took place on the fictitious "Zero Zero Island" (i.e., Null Island), where Earth's equator meets the Greenwich Meridian
* [*Latitude Zero*](/wiki/Latitude_Zero_(film) "Latitude Zero (film)") – a 1969 movie about a fictional [utopia](/wiki/Utopia "Utopia") that is placed at coordinates 0,0 on the bottom of the Gulf of Guinea
